detail: Cycle 8 real-data v2; HYPOTHESIS.md pre-written (revised by Cycle 7); precondition-gated affine GRU, curved-feature PLE, deficit-aware
Synthetic affine-GRU PoC: precondition gate (GRU vs tabular + shuffle) + deficit-aware curved-count PLE vs log-adequate amount

Precondition gate PASS (GRU-log 0.465 > tabular 0.391; shuffle drop +0.191 = uses order). BUT PLE arms lose badly in the GRU: ple_count-log -0.192, deficit-corrected -0.058 (negative). The PLE-in-GRU deficit (12 correlated bins/step into the recurrence, under-resourced: 25 epochs, no reg/early-stop) is far larger than Cycle 7's static ~-0.04 and dominates the curvature signal - exactly cycle 6's 'PLE training-sensitive' + cycle 7's structural deficit compounding. Step 6 must control it heavily.

User correction: retrain GRU under Cycle-6 regime (minibatch bs256 + per-epoch reshuffle, 30 epochs, val split, early-stop patience 6, best-state restore). The prior full-batch 25-step loop undertrained the wide PLE path (12 bins/feat) and produced a spurious -0.192 deficit.
